title: cornus sanguinea
intro: Cornus sanguinea, also known as the blood dogwood or common dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Cornus sanguinea have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various conditions, including wounds, infections, and digestive issues.
